# Project Copilot Instructions

For this workspace, prioritize the skill `joint-sparse-thesis-router` for thesis-related requests.

Apply this default behavior:
- If request is short or ambiguous, infer the most likely thesis workflow and execute directly.
- Prefer minimal executable output first, then expand on demand.
- Avoid repeating previously confirmed project background.
- Keep outputs task-oriented and evidence-based.

When user intent clearly maps to one category, follow this mapping:
- Alignment / gap / off-topic -> gap scan
- Experiment settings / stopping criteria / success rule -> protocol extraction
- Code structure / module split / implementation order -> code cocreation
- Results do not match paper / unstable curves -> debugging
- Write experiment analysis section -> results writing
- Defense prep / expected committee questions -> defense prep
- ARIP / phase transition theory explanation -> theory compression
- Figure reproduction / caption drafting -> figure and caption
